data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-508a0297">
    <title>Integrate prose findings into bead review as opt-in advisory output</title>
    <description>
CONTEXT
This is an explicit follow-up, not part of the first executable slice. Once `ddx doc prose --changed` has landed and advisory noise is known, DDx can expose prose findings inside bead review or a review hook.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/bead_review*.go or existing review command files
- cli/internal/agent/review/lint hook code if TD-036 selects that path
- review tests using prose fixtures
- docs updates for review integration

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- Add an opt-in review surface such as `ddx bead review <id> --prose` or the exact command selected by TD-036.
- Findings remain advisory by default.
- Review output must distinguish prose-quality findings from acceptance/correctness findings.
- The integration must reuse the same checker/rule/config path as `ddx doc prose`.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Making prose findings globally blocking.
- Changing execute-loop selection or closure semantics based only on prose findings.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. A prose review integration command or flag exists as specified by TD-036 and is opt-in.
2. Tests prove review output includes prose findings separately from acceptance/correctness review findings.
3. Tests prove prose findings remain advisory by default and do not close/reopen/block beads on their own.
4. The integration reuses the same config/rule path as `ddx doc prose` rather than duplicating rule logic.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/agent/... ./internal/docprose/... -run "Prose|Review"` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:cli, area:agent, kind:feature, prose-quality, follow-up</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="TD-036" base-rev="3ea39b5e4b986fdeb30349006bd982b58ed359b3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T115251-15c8c0c3"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="TD-036" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md">Technical Design: Prose Quality Pipeline</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Implemented and committed as `53e02f4a7` (`feat(bead-review): add opt-in prose findings [ddx-508a0297]`).

What changed:
- Added `--prose` to bead review in [`cli/cmd/bead_review.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/cmd/bead_review.go).
- Reused the shared prose config path via [`cli/internal/docprose/settings.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/docprose/settings.go), and updated [`cli/cmd/doc_prose.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/cmd/doc_prose.go) to use it.
- Extended bead review prompt generation and result parsing to carry separate prose findings in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go), [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go), and [`cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go).
- Added coverage for the opt-in prompt path, parser, advisory semantics, and shared settings resolution in:
  - [`cli/cmd/bead_review_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/cmd/bead_review_test.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go)
  - [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go)
  - [`cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go)
- Updated docs in [`docs/prose-quality.md`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/docs/prose-quality.md) and [`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-508a0297-20260506T115251-15c8c0c3/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md).
